content.problemStatement:
Kuroni is not only a great programmer, but also a successful farmer. He has already built NNN cowsheds for his cows (numbered 111 through NNN) and equipped them with air conditioners, refrigerators, etc. Despite these facilities and high quality food, the cows do not seem to be happy. Through research, Kuroni figured out that cows also need the Internet to communicate and chit-chat with each other.

Kuroni decided to build a LAN system of connections between his cowsheds. Initially, no connections between the cowsheds exist. Then, for QQQ days (numbered 111 through QQQ), Kuroni adds some direct connections without removing any previously added connections. For each valid iii, on the iii-th day, Kuroni adds direct bidirectional connections between pairs of cowsheds (Li,Ri),(Li+1,Ri−1),…,(Ri,Li)(L_i, R_i), (L_i + 1, R_i - 1), \ldots, (R_i, L_i)(Li​,Ri​),(Li​+1,Ri​−1),…,(Ri​,Li​), i.e. (Li+k,Ri−k)(L_i + k, R_i - k)(Li​+k,Ri​−k) for each 0≤k≤Ri−Li0 \le k \le R_i - L_i0≤k≤Ri​−Li​. (There may be multiple connections between some pairs of cowsheds. A cowshed may also be connected with itself.)

Two cowsheds are in the same group if they can communicate with each other either directly or indirectly ― through connections with some other cowsheds. At the end of each day, tell Kuroni the number of groups of cowsheds that currently exist.
constraints: 1≤Li≤Ri≤N1 \le L_i \le R_i \le N1≤Li​≤Ri​≤N for each valid iii
`Explanation**Example case 1:** On the first, second and third day, one connection between cowsheds (3,5)(3, 5)(3,5), (4,5)(4, 5)(4,5) and (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) is added, respectively.
On the first day, only one connection between cowsheds (3,5)(3, 5)(3,5) is added, so the number of group is 666.

On the second day, connections between pairs of cowsheds (3,7)(3, 7)(3,7) and (4,6)(4, 6)(4,6) are added, so we now have four groups {1}\{1\}{1}, {2}\{2\}{2}, {3,5,7}\{3, 5, 7\}{3,5,7} and {4,6}\{4, 6\}{4,6}.

On the third day, connections between pairs of cowsheds (1,7)(1, 7)(1,7), (2,6)(2, 6)(2,6) and (3,5)(3, 5)(3,5) are added, so at the end, there are only two groups {1,3,5,7}\{1, 3, 5, 7\}{1,3,5,7} and {2,4,6}\{2, 4, 6\}{2,4,6}.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​.
content.outputFormat: Print QQQ lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain a single integer ― the number of groups at the end of the iii-th day.
